events.event_description: Frederick William Backus Coleman presented credentials as Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary, establishing nonresident ministerial relations with Estonia (resident in Riga, Latvia).

evidence.quote: Frederick William Backus Coleman, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1922-09-20. Credentials presented: 1922-11-20. Left Riga 1931-10-20.

events.event_description: Legation Tallinn was established, elevating U.S. representation from nonresident envoy to a resident legation, with Harry E. Carlson as Chargé d'Affaires ad interim.

evidence.quote: Legation Tallinn was established on June 30, 1930, with Harry E. Carlson as Chargé d'Affaires ad interim.

events.event_description: Legation Tallinn was officially closed following Soviet occupation of Tallinn and withdrawal of all U.S. diplomatic officials, ending formal U.S. diplomatic representation in Estonia.

evidence.quote: All U.S. diplomatic officials accredited to Estonia were withdrawn from Tallinn 1940-09-04. [Mission Note] Legation Tallinn was officially closed on September 5, 1940.

events.event_description: Robert C Frasure presented credentials as Chargé d'Affaires ad interim, re-establishing U.S. diplomatic representation in Estonia after the restoration of Estonian independence, with the mission operating as an embassy.

evidence.quote: The United States announced its readiness to re-establish relations with Estonia on September 2, 1991. Robert C Frasure, Chargé d'Affaires ad interim. Credentials presented: 1991-10-02.
